req.title: Account-level transaction limits
req.statement: A customer shall configure daily per-channel limits within regulatory caps; changes take effect after a 2-hour cool-down.
preconditions: authenticated

doc.reasoning: The requirement has three testable dimensions: (1) a valid limit submission stores a pending limit and schedules activation after 2 hours (positive/EP), (2) a limit exceeding the regulatory cap is rejected with HTTP 422 (negative/NEG), (3) a limit exactly at the regulatory cap is accepted (boundary/BVA), and (4) the state transition from pending to active after the cool-down elapses (STC). A decision-table case covering multi-channel submissions is also included. Tests are written against the /accounts/{id}/limits endpoint with a mock-clock or time-travel helper to keep them deterministic.

tests.name: test_valid_limit_submission_stores_pending_limit
preconditions: Customer is authenticated (valid JWT in auth_headers fixture)
Account <ACCOUNT_ID> exists and belongs to the authenticated customer
Requested daily limit (5000) is below the regulatory cap
expected: Response body contains effective_at timestamp approximately 2 hours in the future
Subsequent GET /accounts/{id}/limits returns the pending record
trace: R-FIN-017: customer configures daily per-channel limit
tests.executable:
import pytest
from datetime import datetime, timezone, timedelta

def test_valid_limit_submission_stores_pending_limit(api_client, auth_headers, test_account_id):
    payload = {"channel": "web", "daily_limit": 5000}
    resp = api_client.post(
        f"/accounts/{test_account_id}/limits",
        json=payload,
        headers=auth_headers,
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 202, resp.text
    body = resp.json()
    assert body["status"] == "pending"
    effective_at = datetime.fromisoformat(body["effective_at"])
    now_utc = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
    delta = effective_at - now_utc
    assert timedelta(hours=1, minutes=55) <= delta <= timedelta(hours=2, minutes=5), (
        f"effective_at out of expected range: {delta}"
    )
    get_resp = api_client.get(
        f"/accounts/{test_account_id}/limits",
        headers=auth_headers,
    )
    assert get_resp.status_code == 200
    pending = [r for r in get_resp.json()["limits"] if r["status"] == "pending"]
    assert any(r["daily_limit"] == 5000 and r["channel"] == "web" for r in pending)

tests.name: test_limit_over_regulatory_cap_returns_422
preconditions: Customer is authenticated
Account <ACCOUNT_ID> exists
Regulatory cap for channel=web is known (REGULATORY_CAP fixture)
Requested daily limit exceeds REGULATORY_CAP by 1
actions: Inspect response status and error body
expected: Response body contains error code LIMIT_EXCEEDS_REGULATORY_CAP
No pending limit record created for this channel
trace: R-FIN-017: error pathway - 422 when over regulatory cap
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_limit_over_regulatory_cap_returns_422(api_client, auth_headers, test_account_id, regulatory_cap):
    over_cap = regulatory_cap + 1
    resp = api_client.post(
        f"/accounts/{test_account_id}/limits",
        json={"channel": "web", "daily_limit": over_cap},
        headers=auth_headers,
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 422, resp.text
    body = resp.json()
    assert body.get("error") == "LIMIT_EXCEEDS_REGULATORY_CAP", body
    get_resp = api_client.get(
        f"/accounts/{test_account_id}/limits",
        headers=auth_headers,
    )
    assert get_resp.status_code == 200
    pending = [
        r for r in get_resp.json()["limits"]
        if r["status"] == "pending" and r["channel"] == "web" and r["daily_limit"] == over_cap
    ]
    assert len(pending) == 0, "No pending record should exist for over-cap limit"

tests.name: test_limit_exactly_at_regulatory_cap_is_accepted
preconditions: Customer is authenticated
Account <ACCOUNT_ID> exists
Regulatory cap for channel=mobile is known (REGULATORY_CAP fixture)
Requested daily limit equals REGULATORY_CAP exactly
trace: R-FIN-017: boundary - limit exactly at regulatory cap must be accepted
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_limit_exactly_at_regulatory_cap_is_accepted(api_client, auth_headers, test_account_id, regulatory_cap):
    resp = api_client.post(
        f"/accounts/{test_account_id}/limits",
        json={"channel": "mobile", "daily_limit": regulatory_cap},
        headers=auth_headers,
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 202, resp.text
    body = resp.json()
    assert body["status"] == "pending"
    assert body["daily_limit"] == regulatory_cap
    assert body["channel"] == "mobile"

tests.name: test_pending_limit_becomes_active_after_cooldown
preconditions: Customer is authenticated
Account <ACCOUNT_ID> exists
A pending limit for channel=atm has been submitted (via fixture or setup call)
Mock clock or time-travel helper <advance_clock_fn> is available
actions: POST /accounts/{id}/limits with channel=atm, daily_limit=3000 (creates pending state)
Advance server clock by 2 hours using <advance_clock_fn>
expected: After clock advance: status=active for the submitted limit
trace: R-FIN-017: changes take effect after 2-hour cool-down
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_pending_limit_becomes_active_after_cooldown(
    api_client, auth_headers, test_account_id, advance_clock
):
    post_resp = api_client.post(
        f"/accounts/{test_account_id}/limits",
        json={"channel": "atm", "daily_limit": 3000},
        headers=auth_headers,
    )
    assert post_resp.status_code == 202, post_resp.text
    limit_id = post_resp.json()["limit_id"]
    get_before = api_client.get(
        f"/accounts/{test_account_id}/limits/{limit_id}",
        headers=auth_headers,
    )
    assert get_before.json()["status"] == "pending"
    advance_clock(hours=2)
    get_after = api_client.get(
        f"/accounts/{test_account_id}/limits/{limit_id}",
        headers=auth_headers,
    )
    body = get_after.json()
    assert body["status"] == "active", f"Expected active, got: {body['status']}"
    assert body["daily_limit"] == 3000
    assert body["channel"] == "atm"

tests.name: test_multichannel_limit_decision_table
preconditions: Customer is authenticated
Account <ACCOUNT_ID> exists
Regulatory caps per channel available via regulatory_caps fixture (dict keyed by channel)
Test covers three channels: web, mobile, atm with distinct valid limits
actions: Assert all three return 202 and status=pending
expected: All three POST calls return HTTP 202
All three response bodies contain status=pending
GET /accounts/{id}/limits returns three distinct pending records
trace: R-FIN-017: customer configures daily per-channel limits (multiple channels)
R-FIN-017: pending limit stored for each channel
tests.executable:
import pytest

@pytest.mark.parametrize("channel,daily_limit", [
    ("web", 4000),
    ("mobile", 2000),
    ("atm", 1000),
])
def test_multichannel_limit_decision_table(
    api_client, auth_headers, test_account_id, channel, daily_limit
):
    resp = api_client.post(
        f"/accounts/{test_account_id}/limits",
        json={"channel": channel, "daily_limit": daily_limit},
        headers=auth_headers,
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 202, f"[{channel}] Unexpected status: {resp.text}"
    body = resp.json()
    assert body["status"] == "pending", f"[{channel}] Expected pending, got: {body['status']}"
    assert body["channel"] == channel
    assert body["daily_limit"] == daily_limit
